title: Parasenecio cyclotus
taxonomy: Parasenecio cyclotus is classified in the family Asteraceae. This plant is further divided into the subfamily Asteroideae and tribe Senecioneae. The species Parasenecio cyclotus is known to belong to the genus Parasenecio. It is native to the western United States, specifically in the states of Oregon and Washington. This classification helps in understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of the plant.
uses: Parasenecio cyclotus, also known as Pacific wire lettuce, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. benefits: Parasenecio cyclotus, also known as Western Yarrow, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. properties: Parasenecio cyclotus, also known as Western yarrow, has been used in traditional medicine for its various therapeutic properties. Its leaves and flowers contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe wounds and reduce pain. constituents: Parasenecio cyclotus, also known as western yarrow, contains several active constituents, including sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and volatile oils. These constituents contribute to the plant's medicinal and therapeutic properties. Sesquiterpene lactones have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. intro: Parasenecio cyclotus, also known as western yarrow, is a perennial plant species native to North America, characterized by its fern-like foliage and small white or yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antispasmodic properties, making it beneficial for treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of western yarrow include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and volatile oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Western yarrow is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves, often employed for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects.
